tweets.text:
Miguel Solis-Martinez, of Honduras, was arrested in 2008 for soliciting someone to murder his spouse in Bexar County, Texas. He was sentenced to a decade behind bars.

🔎Solis snuck into the country in 1999 near San Ysidro, Calif., and was put into removal proceedings. An immigration judge granted him voluntary departure in 2002 — in other words, he agreed to leave the country on his own rather than get deported. He didn’t leave though.

In addition to the homicide-related conviction, his rap sheet also includes:
➡️Illegal re-entry conviction
➡️Forgery charge
➡️DUI arrest
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Ricardo Crespo-Ruiz, a criminal illegal alien from Mexico, was convicted in 2002 for VOLUNTARY MANSLAUGHTER and BURGLARY in Los Angeles and deported following his 11-year sentence.

In 2025, he tried driving back into the U.S. with a fake passport he says he purchased for $500. That fail with our @CBP partners cost him another 15 months in prison.

ICE @EROLosAngeles ensured he had a ride upon his release May 8 that will eventually land back on his side of the border.

tweets.text:
Brenda Liliana Rivera-Estrada was arrested by police in Arizona on charges of manslaughter and child abuse, but Maricopa county prosecutors declined to file charges.

ICE officers picked her up pursuant to an honored detainer, and she will be held in ICE detention pending a review of her case by the DOJ’s Executive Office for Immigration Review.

Read more⬇️
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Illegal alien Wenceslao O. Panameno was deported back to El Salvador by ICE in 2017 following a DUI and five-year sentence for RAPING A CHILD in Massachusetts.

Then, a 2025 ICE tipline call led our Boston officers and HSI special agents to a residence where they reintroduced themselves to Panameno, who says he snuck in using a passport with someone else’s name. 

He’s now back in our custody following a year in prison for illegal reentry and will be deported soonest.

📞Your tips make America safer: (866) 347-2423.

tweets.text:
Henry Roldan Perez-Roblero, 21, of Guatemala, faces a felony charge in Florida for allegedly sexually assaulting a child under 12 — a capital felony punishable in the state by death.

ICE issued a detainer with law enforcement in Martin County, Florida, and will take custody of him in the event he is ever released. 

👉Unlike sanctuary jurisdiction officials, Florida officials honor our detainers.

Perez-Roblero illegally entered the United States in Arizona in January 2021 and was released into the country. An immigration judge ordered him removed January 22, 2025.

tweets.text:
Oscar Alberto Pelaez was born in Colombia and immigrated to the U.S. to serve as a priest in California.

➡️1999 | Pelaez submitted an application for naturalization, claiming he never committed a crime for which he had not been arrested. 

➡️2001 | Pelaez became a U.S. citizen. 

➡️2002 | Pelaez was convicted of 13 felony offenses after confessing to repeatedly raping a 14-year-old boy in his parish between 1998 and 2000. 

Thanks to an ICE-initiated case through Operation False Haven, the Department of Justice filed a civil complaint to revoke Pelaez’s citizenship for concealing these crimes while applying for naturalization.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Mexican-born lawful permanent resident Miguel Angel Rodriguez-Ramirez was sentenced to 14 years in prison for continuous sexual abuse of a child under 14-years-old in Redwood City, Calif.

We arrested the child predator at-large May 4 in a targeted operation, and he’ll remain in ICE custody to face an immigration judge’s deportation decision later this month.

tweets.text:
Victor Figueiredo De Araujo, of Brazil, served time for robbery and conspiracy in Philadelphia, but local officials there refused to honor an immigration detainer. They released him from incarceration back into the community.

⚖️He has a final removal order, so ICE will deport him to his home nation.

tweets.text:
🔎Fredis Antonio Gonzalez-Recinos had a green card, but threw it away by sexually abusing a child younger than 13 when he was over 21-years-old. He pleaded guilty in Suffolk County, N.Y., of the crime last year and was sentenced to 10 years probation.

🚨ICE Fugitive Operations officers arrested him May 8 near his last known residence in Mastic, N.Y., during a targeted immigration enforcement operation.

tweets.text:
Despite his 2014 cocaine trafficking conviction and a 2018 final removal order, Cuban illegal alien Pedro Fernandez thought he’d casually catch a flight from Florida to Chicago.

ICE and its Florida TSA and Palm Beach Sheriff’s Office partners thought differently. 

They teamed up to arrest him in a targeted immigration enforcement operation at Palm Beach International Airport May 7, and he’ll soon be deported.

tweets.text:
ICE took custody of Jorge Alberto Ramos-Coreas upon his release from Ironwood State Prison in Blythe, Calif., where he had served time for commiting lewd acts with a child. 

He has a final order of removal based the following charges:
⚖️INA § 237(a)(2)(A)(iii): Alien convicted of sexually abusing a child
⚖️INA § 212(a)(6)(A)(i): Alien present without admission or parole

tweets.text:
Zakaria Songotoua was convicted in absentia and sentenced to 30 years for murder, assault and battery after he fled Burkina Faso before trial. 

As a member of the Presidential Security Regiment, he participated in the attempted 2015 coup d’etat in the African nation that included killing 11 and holding the interim president hostage.

After hiding in the sanctuary city of Bronx, N.Y., our New York officers and special agents tracked him down and returned him to Burkina Faso to face justice.

The U.S. is no safe haven for war criminals, and ICE HSI's Human Rights Violators and War Crimes Center ensures those who engage in such bloodshed anywhere in the world have no place in the United States.
tweets.text_resolved:

After hiding in the sanctuary city of Bronx, N.Y., our New York officers and special agents tracked him down and returned him to Burkina Faso to face justice.

The U.S. is no safe haven for war criminals, and ICE HSI's Human Rights Violators and War Crimes Center ensures those who engage in such bloodshed anywhere in the world have no place in the United States.

tweets.text:
Sanctuary politicians are choosing criminal illegal aliens over public safety by denying undercover license plates and access to driver and vehicle registration information for ICE and CBP.

This reckless policy choice impacts our efforts to investigate cases including:
• Child exploitation
• Rape & kidnapping
• Drug and weapons trafficking
• Sex and forced labor trafficking
• Transnational criminal organizations

Thank you @TheJusticeDept for taking action.

tweets.text:
Law enforcement officials worked together to get Wualter Jesus Travieso Soto, 30, from Texas to Montana, where he faces charges for allegedly raping a child in his care multiple times.

🔎Travieso is an illegal alien from Venezuela, so ICE issued a detainer against him.

ICE officers will work with Montana officials to ensure he ends up in ICE custody instead of back in the community after justice is served on this case.
